title: rubus hirtus subsp. anoplocladus
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. anoplocladus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan dewberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus hirtus plant, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. anoplocladus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and is also used in traditional remedies such as jams, jellies, and wines.
